title: Crispy Greek Tomato Flatbread
description: A plant‑based flatbread with a tangy tomato sauce, crisp edges, and classic Greek toppings, perfect for parties or a quick meal.

ingredients.name: tomato sauce
ingredients.notes: smooth or chunky, seasoned with garlic and oregano

instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
2. Add warm water and 2 tbsp olive oil; stir until dough forms.
3. Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth.
4. Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 30 minutes until doubled.
5. Preheat your oven to 500°F (260°C) with a baking stone or pizza stone inside; if you have a tandoor, preheat until the walls are smoking hot.
6. Punch down the dough and divide it into 4 equal portions; roll each into a thin circle (about 1/8 inch thick).
7. Lightly brush each flatbread with olive oil and spread a thin layer of tomato sauce over the surface.
8. Sprinkle plant‑based feta, olives, onion slices, and oregano on top; drizzle a little more oil if desired.
9. Transfer the flatbreads onto the preheated stone or a baking sheet and bake for 8–10 minutes until edges are golden and crisp; return to the tandoor for 2 minutes for a smoky finish if using.
10. Remove, garnish with chopped parsley, slice, and serve warm.
